kills: Conjecture 3 (One-IVT Witness) size bound is near-certainly false as stated: it implies a <= 49-vertex 5-chromatic UDG (H 6-critical and H-w realizable forces chi(H-w) = 5 exactly) that moreover FLEXES, while the smallest known chi-5 UDG is 199-509 vertices and every known one is rigid. A 4x size reduction plus first-ever flexible chi-5 witness, asserted with zero exhibited example, fails the no-buffer prior outright.

Conjecture 3's 'blanket Mycielski non-realizability' is not a conjecture or bet: it is a one-line theorem. Verified computationally here: K_{2,3} is a subgraph of M^3(C5)-w for ALL 47 choices of split vertex w (pairs (v, v') share N(v), 3+ common neighbors), and K_{2,3} is not unit-distance realizable (two unit circles meet in <= 2 points, the repo's own L42/L53 fact). So every Mycielski clamp at every tower height >= 2 and every split is provably non-realizable; test (c) flex-tracking numerics is pointless; the novelty claim collapses for this part. Byproduct for VERIFIER: this upgrades L52's 'evidence of non-realizability' of the 48-vtx clamp to proof.

Conjecture 3's auxiliary necessity claim deg_H(w) <= flex(H-w) + 4 (plus 'flex <= 2 empirically') is DOF/degree counting re-dressed, a direct collision with the L52 kill (P_510 realizes at +1487 over-determination, degree 36) and with the KNOWN KILLS prohibition on re-proposing such heuristics. The proposal even self-labels it 'anti-miracle bet', which is exactly the move L52 invalidated.

Conjecture 3's falsification clause (b) is not runnable: it requires the FULL family of K4-free 6-critical graphs at n <= 50; no enumeration technology exists anywhere near that size, and this host has no C compiler / nauty (L51 already hit this wall at n = 8). So the conjecture is unfalsifiable by its own stated test. Worse, its predictions test the wrong necessary conditions: prediction (a) (degree-5 vertex exists) is already trivially satisfied by the repo's own M^3(C5) (min degree 5, verified here), while the binding necessary condition, K_{2,3}-freeness of H-w, is never mentioned, and not one K4-free 6-critical K_{2,3}-free graph is exhibited at any size.

CCC's only runnable test (a) has essentially zero discriminating power: eliminants of random mechanisms have generic (S_n-type, hence non-2-group) Galois groups whether or not CCC is true, so the predicted 'overwhelming majority non-2-group' outcome confirms nothing. Tests (b) and (c) are contingent on objects that do not exist yet (candidate H-w realizations; a successful pipeline clamp). CCC as packaged is unfalsifiable in the near term despite its 'falsifiable_prediction' framing.

CCC's threshold-at-E is under-determined by its own mechanism: 'no mechanism forces solvability by square roots' argues equally for a non-solvable-by-radicals threshold or any fixed-field threshold; nothing in the IVT story singles out the quadratic closure. The supporting co-occurrence (the entire forcing-free corpus is constructible, L45/L57) is confounded by selection: the corpus is constructible because all prior searches were compass-style. IVT proves the tuned root exists in R; it knows no Galois theory and cannot place the root outside E.

SSL's universal claims are over-reached with a concrete unaddressed threat: O'Donnell-type triangle-free (hence spindle-free and Golomb-free) 4-chromatic UDGs may be flexible, and a positive-dimensional realization curve defined over Q carries dense small-field points, which would kill SSL(ii) wholesale. The spindle probe tests only one rigid instance. Separately SSL(i)'s upper half (chi(Q(sqrt3)^2) <= 3) has zero technology behind it (the proposed ramified-prime valuation 3-coloring is pure speculation; 2 ramifies in Z[sqrt3], v_p(2) = 2, and Woodall's parity mechanism has no known 3-color analog) and possible Geombinatorics prior art on quadratic extensions is un-surveyed.

SSL(ii) literal statement bug: 'every unit-distance realization of the Moser spindle generates a field of degree exactly 4' is false as written (translate by pi: transcendental coordinates; rotate by a high-degree unit vector: arbitrarily large fields). Must be restated as 'the minimal field hosting a spindle realization has degree 4'. The experiment's gauge-fixed formulation is the correct form, but the conjecture text is not.

Experiment precedent misread and soundness gap: h2_groebner_moser14.py never ran a 14-vertex Groebner basis; it linearized bridge-difference equations to 4 unknowns (c, s, tx, ty) and found a rational inconsistency certificate (verified by reading the file). A naive 10-variable lex Groebner over QQ in sympy is far heavier than the cited precedent and the 1-4 CPU-hour estimate is unsupported. Also the design omits the injectivity filter: the gauged polynomial system can have degenerate solutions (coincident non-adjacent vertices) lying in small fields, which would falsely register as a quadratic realization and wrongly 'kill SSL'. Both fixable (resultant/triangular cascade exploiting the spindle's constructive structure; pairwise-distinctness check per solution).

Axiom and complexity hygiene: the framing identity chi(R^2) = chi(A^2) = sup_F chi(F^2) uses de Bruijn-Erdos, i.e., AC, never flagged (Arch-4 rule: a chi(R^2) claim without an axiom set is meaningless here; the repo's own L54 dark horse lives exactly in this gap). And 'EXPSPACE Tarski default' misstates complexity: fixed existential sentences over R are in PSPACE (Canny/Renegar); doubly-exponential behavior belongs to quantifier-alternating CAD.
prior_art_collisions: Woodall 1973 (chi(Q^2) = 2): built in as the staircase ground step, acknowledged.
Tarski transfer chi(R^2) = chi(A^2) and finite-witness reduction: folklore (Soifer's book, Polymath16 discussions); requires AC via de Bruijn-Erdos, which the proposal does not state.

Polymath16 ring threads (chromatic numbers of Z[omega_1], Z[omega_1, omega_3], ...): the grade-by-arithmetic idea is theirs; SSL/CCC's residual novelty is only the degree-minimality claims d(4) = d(5) = 4 and the quadratic-closure threshold.

Possible Geombinatorics literature (P. D. Johnson Jr. and co-authors) on chromatic numbers of quadratic extensions Q(sqrt d)^2: may already settle or contradict SSL(i); the proposal itself hedges; SURVEYOR must check before any SSL logging.

Classical K_{2,3} forbidden-subgraph fact for unit-distance graphs (two unit circles meet in <= 2 points; repo's own L42/L53): already proves the Mycielski clamp non-realizability that Conjecture 3 frames as a novel bet, and proves it for every split vertex (verified here computationally).
O'Donnell 1995-2000: triangle-free 4-chromatic UDGs of arbitrary girth: the standing counterexample family threatening SSL(ii); their field/flexibility data is the real test, not the spindle.

Hauenstein-Sottile alphaCertified and standard validated numerics (interval Newton/Krawczyk, certified path tracking): the 'IVT certificate normal form' is repackaged validated continuation; cited by the proposal but the packaging is the only new part.
Exoo-Ismailescu / Heule small chi-5 UDG record (~509-517 verified; repo lineage floor 199): calibrates the implausibility of Conjecture 3's n <= 50 bound.

Spencer-Szemeredi-Trotter unit-distance bound and Agoston-Palvolgyi exact small-n maxima: edge-density sanity check; a 6-critical H at n <= 50 forces H-w edge counts at or beyond the densest known unit-distance configurations.
result.surviving_kernel: Three things survive. (1) The field-degree staircase d(k) as a named, well-posed bookkeeping object: d(3) = 2 is already a theorem (Woodall plus the area-determinant argument that any unit equilateral triangle in F^2 forces sqrt3 into F, no gauge needed), and the spindle field-degree computation, after the injectivity fix, gives the first proven instance above Woodall. (2) The strategy corollary, which is the proposal's real content: stop compass/tower-restricted clamp search; run numerical flex continuation on candidate H-w (filtered FIRST by K_{2,3}-freeness, the binding necessary condition the proposal missed), monitor the Theorem-R circumradius along flexes, and certify a posteriori (interval IVT sign change, PSLQ field recovery, exact verification in the recovered field). This is sound, repo-feasible, detector-clean for the right reasons, and is the only route consistent with L55's wide-interface verdict. (3) The W3 certificate normal form (square-root-tower arc presentation, interval validation of separations and radicands, one sign change) as a verification standard for whenever a candidate materializes. Bonus byproduct independent of the proposal: the K_{2,3} scan proves the 48-vertex Mycielski clamp non-realizable for every split, upgrading L52 from evidence to theorem and adding K_{2,3}-freeness as a hard filter for all future clamp candidates.
result.cheapest_decisive_test: Two-step, both under an hour. Step 0 (done in this report, 10 lines of Python): K_{2,3} common-neighbor scan on M^3(C5)-w for all w; result positive for all 47 splits, retiring Conjecture 3's test (c) and the Mycielski 'bet' as a theorem. Step 1 (decides the only provably decidable claim in the proposal, SSL's spindle instance, both ways): gauge-fix the spindle on edge (p1, p2), solve the 10-unknown system by triangular/resultant cascade (rhombus 1, rhombus 2, hinge tie) instead of naive lex Groebner, filter solutions by pairwise vertex distinctness, then compute the minimal polynomial degree of the field generated by each surviving solution. Output: either every faithful solution generates a degree-4 field (first proven staircase riser above Woodall) or a quadratic-field solution exists and SSL dies immediately.
result.steelman: Strip the three conjectures to one conservative bet plus a search policy. Bet: the minimal field hosting any chi >= 6 finite UDG witness (equivalently any realizable clamp, via Theorem R and the L24 lift) is not contained in the quadratic closure E of Q in R; operationally, the W3 tuning eliminant of a flexed H-w is irreducible with non-2-group Galois closure. State it explicitly under ZFC, as a prediction about where the witness lives, not as a theorem target, and drop the unsupported companion claims (n <= 50, deg <= flex + 4, chi(E^2) = 5's inaccessible upper half, chi(Q(sqrt3)^2) = 3's upper half). Policy: (i) log d(k) as a permanent object; prove its first riser by the fixed spindle computation; (ii) build the clamp-candidate pipeline with the necessary-condition filters in the right order: K_{2,3}-free first (binding, free to check), then K4-free 6-critical with a degree-5 vertex, with NO size cap; (iii) hunt flexes numerically on surviving H-w and certify any circumradius sign change a posteriori via the interval-IVT plus PSLQ normal form, which is exactly where verification is cheap and elimination is hopeless. This version collides with no known kill, passes all three detectors for structural reasons (IVT needs completeness of R, circumradius and two-point circle intersection are Euclidean-specific, flex arcs are rotational), and converts the proposal's arithmetic intuition into pure search guidance with millisecond-checkable certificates.
